content.text: Long, long ago, when the world was new, the first man and the first woman lived together in a beautiful land.
They were very happy, for they had everything they needed—warm sunlight, cool water, and the sweetest fruits growing all around them.
The man loved the woman dearly, and the woman loved the man just as much.
One morning, the man went out to gather food for their breakfast.
He picked the ripest berries, the juiciest grapes, and the most tender greens.
When he returned, he placed the food before the woman, but she was not pleased.
She had been waiting for him, and she felt he had taken too long.
Her heart was filled with anger, and without thinking, she spoke harshly to him.
The man was hurt by her words.
He had worked hard to bring her food, and now she was ungrateful.
His own heart grew heavy with sadness, and without another word, he turned and walked away into the forest.
The woman watched him go, and as she stood there alone, her anger began to fade.
She realized she had been wrong to speak so sharply.
She missed the man already, and she wished she could take back her words.
But he was gone, and she did not know where to find him.
The Sun, who had been watching all this from the sky, felt sorry for the woman.
The Sun knew how much the man and woman loved each other, and it did not want them to be apart.
So the Sun decided to help.
The Sun shone its golden light upon the earth where the man had walked.
And where the Sun’s rays touched the ground, something wonderful happened.
The first strawberries began to grow—small, red, and glistening like tiny jewels.
They were the sweetest berries the world had ever known, and their scent filled the air with a delicious fragrance.
The woman saw the strawberries and bent down to pick one.
As she tasted it, the sweetness filled her mouth, and her heart grew lighter.
She remembered how much she loved the man, and she knew she had to find him.
She began to follow the path where the strawberries grew, picking them as she went.
The man, too, had stopped to rest beneath a tree.
He was still sad, but the forest was quiet and peaceful. Then, he heard a soft rustling sound.
He turned and saw the woman coming toward him, holding out a strawberry.
Her eyes were filled with kindness, and she smiled at him. “I’m sorry,” she said softly.
“I should not have spoken to you that way. Will you forgive me?”  The man’s heart swelled with love.
He took the strawberry from her hand and ate it.
The sweetness filled him with joy, and he knew he could not stay angry.
“Of course I forgive you,” he said.
“And I am sorry too, for walking away.”  The woman smiled, and the man reached out to take her hand.
Together, they walked back to their home, following the trail of strawberries.
And from that day on, whenever people saw strawberries growing, they remembered the story of the first man and woman, and how the sweetness of the berries helped heal their hearts.
And so, strawberries became a sign of love, forgiveness, and the sweetness that comes when we make things right with those we care about.
